data.text: The current shape is clearer now: `RunWithRateLimitRetry` already owns the policy in `ratelimit_retry.go`, while `try.Attempt` only handles conflict/no-changes outcomes. I’m checking the loop caller and test surface next to decide whether the clean move is to have `try.Attempt` return a retry-aware outcome or to embed the wrapper inside the attempt executor path.

The execute-bead worker already has the hook plumbing and only needs the retry decision and loop to relocate. I’m reading the try adapter now because that’s the cleanest place to keep `execute_bead.go` generic while still letting `try.Attempt` own the retry boundary.

package agent

import (
	"context"

// Config holds agent service configuration.
type Config struct {
	Profile         string              `yaml:"profile"`          // default routing intent: default, cheap, fast, smart
	Harness         string              `yaml:"harness"`          // optional forced harness override
	Model           string              `yaml:"model"`            // optional default model ref or exact pin
	Models          map[string]string   `yaml:"models"`           // per-harness model overrides
	ReasoningLevels map[string][]string `yaml:"reasoning_levels"` // per-harness reasoning-level options
	TimeoutMS       int                 `yaml:"timeout_ms"`       // idle (inactivity) timeout in ms — resets on every stream/event
	WallClockMS     int                 `yaml:"wall_clock_ms"`    // absolute wall-clock cap in ms — fires regardless of activity
	SessionLogDir   string              `yaml:"session_log_dir"`  // log directory
	Permissions     string              `yaml:"permissions"`      // permission level: safe, supervised, unrestricted
}

// RouteFlags holds raw CLI flag values before normalization into a RouteRequest.
// These come directly from parsed command-line arguments.
type RouteFlags struct {
	Profile     string // --profile: default, cheap, fast, smart
	Model       string // --model: logical ref or exact pin
	Provider    string // --provider: explicit provider name
	ModelRef    string // --model-ref: catalog model reference
	Harness     string // --harness: forced harness override
	Effort      string // --effort: low, medium, high
	Permissions string // --permissions: safe, supervised, unrestricted
}

// RunArgs holds options for a single agent invocation.
type RunArgs struct {
	// Context is the caller's context. When non-nil, the Runner derives its
	// internal cancel context from this so upstream cancellation (e.g.
	// server.WorkerManager.Stop) propagates into the provider HTTP call.
	// Nil defaults to context.Background().
	Context       context.Context
	Harness       string
	Prompt        string // prompt text (or path to file)
	PromptFile    string // explicit file path
	PromptSource  string
	Correlation   map[string]string
	Model         string
	Provider      string // explicit provider name (e.g. "vidar", "openrouter"); bypasses default provider selection
	ModelRef      string // catalog model-ref (e.g. "code-medium"); resolved via the model catalog
	Effort        string
	Timeout       time.Duration // idle (inactivity) timeout; nonzero overrides Config.TimeoutMS
	WallClock     time.Duration // absolute wall-clock cap; nonzero overrides Config.WallClockMS
	WorkDir       string
	Permissions   string // permission level override: safe, supervised, unrestricted
	SessionLogDir string // per-run override for session log dir; used by execute-bead to redirect embedded-agent runtime state out of the worktree root
	Role          string
	CorrelationID string
}

// AgentRunRuntime is the SD-024 successor to RunArgs for the agent
// run path. Durable knobs (Harness, Model, Provider, ModelRef, Effort,
// Permissions, Timeout, WallClock, SessionLogDir) are stripped — they
// live on config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via RunWithConfig's
// rcfg argument. Only non-serializable plumbing and per-invocation
// runtime intent remain.
//
// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Runtime structs and §Stage 2 bead 17.
type AgentRunRuntime struct {
	Prompt                string
	PromptFile            string
	PromptSource          string
	Output                io.Writer
	Correlation           map[string]string
	Record                bool
	Replay                bool
	WorkDir               string
	EstimatedPromptTokens int
	RequiresTools         bool
	SessionLogDirOverride string
	// HarnessOverride, ModelOverride, and PermissionsOverride let a caller
	// pin one of the durable knobs from rcfg for this single invocation

   117	func Attempt(ctx context.Context, store Store, beadID string, opts AttemptOpts) (Outcome, error) {
   118		if opts.Executor == nil {
   119			return Outcome{}, fmt.Errorf("try attempt: executor is required")
   120		}
   121		rateCfg := resolveRateLimitRetryConfig(opts)
   122		var (
   123			report Report
   124			err    error
   125		)
   126		for retryCount, elapsed := 0, time.Duration(0); ; {
   127			report, err = opts.Executor.Execute(ctx, beadID)
   128			if err != nil {
   129				report = Report{
   130					BeadID: beadID,
   131					Status: StatusExecutionFailed,
   132					Detail: err.Error(),
   133					Error:  err.Error(),
   134				}
   135			}
   136			if report.BeadID == "" {
   137				report.BeadID = beadID
   138			}
   139			if report.Status == "" {
   140				report.Status = StatusExecutionFailed
   141			}
   142	
   143			rateLimited := IsRateLimitReport(&report)
   144			effectiveBudget := rateCfg.Budget
   145			if opts.RateLimitBudget == 0 && report.RateLimitBudget != 0 {
   146				effectiveBudget = report.RateLimitBudget
   147			}
   148			decision, retryAfter := evaluateRateLimitAttempt(report, rateCfg, retryCount+1, elapsed, effectiveBudget)
   149			if !decision.ShouldRetry {
   150				if decision.Reason != "" {
   151					report.Error = decision.Reason
   152				}
   153				if rateLimited && decision.Reason != "" && rateCfg.OnRetry != nil {
   154					rateCfg.OnRetry(ctx, RateLimitRetryInfo{
   155						Attempt:    retryCount + 1,
   156						Source:     decision.Source,
   157						Report:     &report,
   158						Elapsed:    elapsed,
   159						RetryAfter: retryAfter,
   160						OverBudget: true,
   161					})
   162				}
   163				break
   164			}
   165	
   166			retryCount++
   167			info := RateLimitRetryInfo{
   168				Attempt:    retryCount,
   169				Wait:       decision.Wait,
   170				Source:     decision.Source,
   171				Report:     &report,
   172				Elapsed:    elapsed,
   173				RetryAfter: retryAfter,
   174			}
   175			if rateCfg.OnRetry != nil {
   176				rateCfg.OnRetry(ctx, info)
   177			}
   178			if err := rateCfg.Sleep(ctx, decision.Wait); err != nil {
   179				return Outcome{}, err
   180			}
   181			elapsed += decision.Wait
   182		}
   183	
   184		if ShouldAttemptConflictRecovery(report, opts.ProjectRoot) {
   185			if opts.Store == nil {
   186				opts.Store = store
   187			}
   188			return ConflictRecoveryOutcome(ctx, ConflictRecoveryInput{
   189				Bead:             opts.Bead,
   190				Report:           report,
   191				ProjectRoot:      opts.ProjectRoot,
   192				AutoRecover:      opts.AutoRecover,
   193				ConflictResolver: opts.ConflictResolver,
   194				Store:            opts.Store,
   195				Assignee:         opts.Assignee,
   196				Now:              opts.Now,
   197				Cooldown:         opts.Cooldown,
   198			}), err
   199		}
   200	
   201		if report.Status == StatusNoChanges {
   202			if opts.Store == nil {
   203				opts.Store = store
   204			}
   205			if opts.Store == nil {
   206				return Outcome{Report: report, StoreErrOp: "IncrNoChangesCount", StoreErr: fmt.Errorf("try attempt: no_changes store is required")}, nil
   207			}
   208			noChangesCount, countErr := opts.Store.IncrNoChangesCount(beadID)
   209			if countErr != nil {
   210				return Outcome{Report: report, StoreErrOp: "IncrNoChangesCount", StoreErr: countErr}, nil
   211			}
   212			noChangesOut, adjudicatedReport, adjErr := adjudicateNoChangesContract(ctx, beadID, report, opts.ProjectRoot, noChangesCount, opts.SatisfactionChecker, opts.VerificationRunner)
   213			if adjErr != nil {
   214				return Outcome{Report: report, StoreErrOp: "adjudicateNoChanges", StoreErr: adjErr}, nil
   215			}
   216			return Outcome{
   217				Report:      adjudicatedReport,
   218				Disposition: OutcomeReported,
   219				NoChanges:   &noChangesOut,
   220			}, nil
   221		}
   222	
   223		return Outcome{Report: report, Disposition: OutcomeReported}, err
   224	}

What changed:
- `try.Attempt` now owns the rate-limit retry loop, `Retry-After` parsing, exponential backoff fallback, budget enforcement, and retry hooks in [cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-e36d7959-20260505T165234-0c0b5dec/cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go#L117).

- Adapter plumbing now preserves those fields through the try bridge in [cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_try_adapter.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-e36d7959-20260505T165234-0c0b5dec/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_try_adapter.go#L9) and [cli/internal/agent/integration_helper_test.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-e36d7959-20260505T165234-0c0b5dec/cli/internal/agent/integration_helper_test.go#L218).
- Added focused coverage for retry-after, exponential backoff, and evaluator wiring in [cli/internal/agent/try/attempt_test.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-e36d7959-20260505T165234-0c0b5dec/cli/internal/agent/try/attempt_test.go#L156).
